text: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into oxygen and energy in the form of sugar. This fundamental biochemical reaction provides the primary nourishment for the organism and oxygen for the atmosphere. Britannica +2The entire process takes place in two main stages inside the cell's chloroplasts: National Institutes of Health (.gov) +1The Light-Dependent Reactions: Occurring in the thylakoid membranes, chlorophyll absorbs sunlight and uses its energy to split water molecules (H2Ocap H sub 2 cap O𝐻2𝑂). This releases oxygen into the air, while generating ATP and NADPH, which act as cellular energy carriers.The Light-Independent Reactions (The Calvin Cycle): Occurring in the stroma (the fluid of the chloroplast), this step does not require light. It uses the ATP and NADPH generated in the first stage to convert carbon dioxide (CO2cap C cap O sub 2𝐶𝑂2) from the air into glucose. National Institutes of Health (.gov) +2For a detailed, animated breakdown of how the light and dark reactions operate step-by-step, check out this video:8mPhotosynthesis: Crash Course Biology #8CrashCourseYouTube• 20 mar 2012
